tables.name: SI_ExecutionTracking
tables.description: Per-workflow execution tracking for IBM Sterling B2B Integrator FA_CLIENTS_MAIN runs. One row per WORKFLOW_ID capturing the full b2bi-side picture of a run: core execution identity and timing, workflow tree linkage (parent/root), all 73 ProcessData configuration fields parsed from the MAIN run's <Result><Client> block, derived classification (run_class), raw decompressed ProcessData XML for forensic drill-down, failure detail from WORKFLOW_CONTEXT, and sub-workflow invocation summary. The is_complete flag drives a collector anti-join that excludes terminal rows from future cycles.
tables.dataFlow: Populated by Collect-B2BExecution.ps1 against b2bi on FA-INT-DBP. Each cycle: the collector determines a lookback window from GlobalConfig (b2b_collect_lookback_days, default 3), queries WF_INST_S joined to WFD to find FA_CLIENTS_MAIN runs within the window, anti-joins against already-terminal rows in this table (is_complete = 1), and processes the remainder. For each target workflow it reads WORKFLOW_LINKAGE for parent/root IDs, fetches and decompresses the first DOCUMENT TRANS_DATA row to get ProcessData, parses the XML into the 73 structured columns, scans WORKFLOW_CONTEXT to compute sub-workflow invocation flags and step counts, evaluates terminal state from END_TIME/STATUS, and writes the row via INSERT (new) or UPDATE (still-in-flight from prior cycle).

columns.name: workflow_id
columns.description: Primary key. WORKFLOW_ID from b2bi.dbo.WF_INST_S. Sterling-assigned identifier for a FA_CLIENTS_MAIN execution; equals Integration.etl.tbl_B2B_* RUN_ID.

columns.name: workflow_start_time
columns.description: START_TIME from b2bi.dbo.WF_INST_S. When Sterling launched this MAIN workflow.

columns.name: workflow_end_time
columns.description: END_TIME from b2bi.dbo.WF_INST_S. When the workflow reached a terminal state. NULL while in-flight.

columns.name: duration_ms
columns.description: Workflow runtime in milliseconds, computed from END_TIME - START_TIME. NULL while in-flight.

columns.name: b2bi_status
columns.description: STATUS from b2bi.dbo.WF_INST_S. Sterling status code: 0 = completed successfully, 1 = terminated with errors. NULL while in-flight.

columns.name: b2bi_state
columns.description: STATE from b2bi.dbo.WF_INST_S. Sterling internal state code (observed uniformly as 1 on terminal rows). Captured for lineage completeness.

columns.name: step_count
columns.description: Total number of WORKFLOW_CONTEXT step rows recorded for this workflow. Rough proxy for execution complexity.

columns.name: parent_workflow_id
columns.description: P_WF_ID from b2bi.dbo.WORKFLOW_LINKAGE. The immediate parent workflow (typically the DISPATCHER that launched this MAIN). NULL for top-level workflows with no parent.

columns.name: root_workflow_id
columns.description: ROOT_WF_ID from b2bi.dbo.WORKFLOW_LINKAGE. Highest ancestor in the workflow tree. Used for pipeline rollup ("all MAIN runs under this dispatcher").

columns.name: client_id
columns.description: CLIENT_ID from ProcessData. Identifies the client this run services. Client 328 (INTEGRATION TOOLS) is a pseudo-client for internal SP-executor runs.

columns.name: seq_id
columns.description: SEQ_ID from ProcessData. Identifies the configured process variant within the client. A single client can have multiple SEQ_IDs for different file types or pipelines.

columns.name: client_name
columns.description: CLIENT_NAME from ProcessData. Display name of the client.

columns.name: process_type
columns.description: PROCESS_TYPE from ProcessData. Named configuration pattern (e.g., SPECIAL_PROCESS, SFTP_PUSH_ED25519, EMAIL_SCRUB, NEW_BUSINESS, PAYMENT, NOTES_OUTBOUND).

columns.name: comm_method
columns.description: COMM_METHOD from ProcessData. Direction and transport class: INBOUND, OUTBOUND, or a specific transport name.

columns.name: file_id
columns.description: FILE_ID from ProcessData. Sterling-side file identifier used in file_registry lookup. May be 0 for runs that do not process files.

columns.name: pre_archive
columns.description: PRE_ARCHIVE flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether source files are archived before translation.

columns.name: translation_map
columns.description: TRANSLATION_MAP from ProcessData. Sterling map name that transforms the source file to the target format.

columns.name: workers_comp
columns.description: WORKERS_COMP flag from ProcessData. Y/N — workers' compensation flag, client-specific semantics.

columns.name: dup_check
columns.description: DUP_CHECK from ProcessData. Either a Y/N flag or the name of a duplicate-check SP to invoke.

columns.name: prepare_source
columns.description: PREPARE_SOURCE flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether the preparation pipeline step runs before translation.

columns.name: post_archive
columns.description: POST_ARCHIVE flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether post-translation output is archived.

columns.name: file_filter
columns.description: FILE_FILTER from ProcessData. File name pattern or XPath filter expression the workflow uses to identify source files. Can be very long for complex filters.

columns.name: mail_to
columns.description: MAIL_TO from ProcessData. Comma-separated primary email recipient list for workflow notifications. NULL when no email dispatch is configured.

columns.name: mail_cc
columns.description: MAIL_CC from ProcessData. Comma-separated CC email recipient list. NULL when no CC is configured.

columns.name: get_docs_type
columns.description: GET_DOCS_TYPE from ProcessData. Method for picking up inbound files: SFTP_PULL, FSA_PULL, API_PULL, etc. Populated presence is a FILE_PROCESS classification signal.

columns.name: get_docs_loc
columns.description: GET_DOCS_LOC from ProcessData. Source location (path, URL, or endpoint identifier) where inbound files are picked up.

columns.name: put_docs_type
columns.description: PUT_DOCS_TYPE from ProcessData. Method for delivering outbound files: SFTP_PUSH, FSA_PUSH, etc. Populated presence is a FILE_PROCESS classification signal.

columns.name: put_docs_loc
columns.description: PUT_DOCS_LOC from ProcessData. Destination location where outbound files are delivered.

columns.name: file_rename
columns.description: FILE_RENAME from ProcessData. Either a Y/N flag or an XPath expression that computes the rename target.

columns.name: prepare_comm_call
columns.description: PREPARE_COMM_CALL flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether the pre-communication preparation step runs.

columns.name: comm_call
columns.description: COMM_CALL flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether the communication-call step fires in the pipeline.

columns.name: business_type
columns.description: BUSINESS_TYPE from ProcessData. Business domain classifier: BD (business data), EO (early-out), EOBD, BDEO. Distinguishes pipeline variants for the same client.

columns.name: convert_to_csv
columns.description: CONVERT_TO_CSV from ProcessData. Either a Y/N flag or the delimiter character for conversion.

columns.name: auto_release
columns.description: AUTO_RELEASE flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether batches created by the run are automatically released for processing.

columns.name: put_docs_profile_id
columns.description: PUT_DOCS_PROFILE_ID from ProcessData. Sterling transport profile identifier for outbound delivery.

columns.name: sql_query
columns.description: SQL_QUERY from ProcessData. User-authored SQL the workflow runs (typically for data sourcing). Can be very long for complex queries.

columns.name: mdos
columns.description: MDOS from ProcessData. Medical date-of-service identifier. Format and usage vary by client.

columns.name: send_empty_files
columns.description: SEND_EMPTY_FILES flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether empty output files are sent or suppressed.

columns.name: get_docs_dlt
columns.description: GET_DOCS_DLT from ProcessData. Either a Y/N flag or an archive path for delete-after-pickup behavior.

columns.name: prep_translation_map
columns.description: PREP_TRANSLATION_MAP from ProcessData. Optional preparation-phase map name, applied before the main translation_map.

columns.name: gzip
columns.description: GZIP flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether output files are gzip-compressed.

columns.name: email_xslt
columns.description: EMAIL_XSLT from ProcessData. Reference to the XSLT stylesheet used to render email body content.

columns.name: pgp_passphrase
columns.description: PGP_PASSPHRASE from ProcessData. PGP passphrase stored in plaintext in the Sterling configuration.

columns.name: unzip_filter
columns.description: UNZIP_FILTER from ProcessData. File pattern applied when extracting archive files.

columns.name: unzip_password
columns.description: UNZIP_PASSWORD from ProcessData. Password for zip extraction, stored in plaintext in the Sterling configuration.

columns.name: encounter_map
columns.description: ENCOUNTER_MAP from ProcessData. Named map for healthcare encounter data transformation.

columns.name: pv_fn_address
columns.description: PV_FN_ADDRESS from ProcessData. Address validation behavior enum (e.g., when and how addresses are checked).

columns.name: get_docs_profile_id
columns.description: GET_DOCS_PROFILE_ID from ProcessData. Sterling transport profile identifier for inbound pickup.

columns.name: pdf_file
columns.description: PDF_FILE flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether the workflow handles PDF file outputs.

columns.name: custom_bp_pre_translation
columns.description: CUSTOM_BP_PRE_TRANSLATION from ProcessData. Name of a custom Sterling business process invoked before the main translation phase.

columns.name: custom_bp_pre_process
columns.description: CUSTOM_BP_PRE_PROCESS from ProcessData. Name of a custom Sterling business process invoked before any processing.

columns.name: sql_query_data_source
columns.description: SQL_QUERY_DATA_SOURCE from ProcessData. Named data source (JDBC pool reference) used to execute sql_query.

columns.name: prep_encode_from
columns.description: PREP_ENCODE_FROM from ProcessData. Source encoding name for the preparation phase encoding conversion.

columns.name: prep_encode_to
columns.description: PREP_ENCODE_TO from ProcessData. Target encoding name for the preparation phase encoding conversion.

columns.name: post_translation
columns.description: POST_TRANSLATION flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether the post-translation step runs.

columns.name: post_trans_sql_query
columns.description: POST_TRANS_SQL_QUERY from ProcessData. SQL statement or XPath concat() expression executed in the post-translation phase. Values starting with "EXEC Integration." or "concat('EXEC Integration." indicate SP-executor runs (INTERNAL_OP classification).

columns.name: post_translation_map
columns.description: POST_TRANSLATION_MAP from ProcessData. Sterling map name used in the post-translation step.

columns.name: cla_exe_path
columns.description: CLA_EXE_PATH from ProcessData. Path to an external executable invoked by the workflow.

columns.name: pre_keyword_replace_from
columns.description: PRE_KEYWORD_REPLACE_FROM from ProcessData. Keyword to replace in content during pre-translation text substitution.

columns.name: pre_keyword_replace_to
columns.description: PRE_KEYWORD_REPLACE_TO from ProcessData. Replacement value paired with pre_keyword_replace_from.

columns.name: post_keyword_replace_from
columns.description: POST_KEYWORD_REPLACE_FROM from ProcessData. Keyword to replace in content during post-translation text substitution.

columns.name: post_keyword_replace_to
columns.description: POST_KEYWORD_REPLACE_TO from ProcessData. Replacement value paired with post_keyword_replace_from.

columns.name: post_translation_overwrite
columns.description: POST_TRANSLATION_OVERWRITE flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether post-translation writes overwrite existing output.

columns.name: misc_rec1
columns.description: MISC_REC1 from ProcessData. Miscellaneous delimited-string field for client-specific extra configuration. Semantics vary.

columns.name: get_docs_api
columns.description: GET_DOCS_API from ProcessData. Path to the API-pickup Python executable (or equivalent script path) when pickup uses a custom API invocation.

columns.name: post_translation_vital
columns.description: POST_TRANSLATION_VITAL flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether post-translation failure fails the whole run or is treated as non-fatal.

columns.name: get_empty_docs
columns.description: GET_EMPTY_DOCS flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether empty source files are processed or skipped at pickup.

columns.name: email_subject
columns.description: EMAIL_SUBJECT from ProcessData. Often an XPath concat() expression that builds the subject line at runtime from workflow context.

columns.name: post_trans_file_rename
columns.description: POST_TRANS_FILE_RENAME from ProcessData. Either a Y/N flag or an XPath-driven filename pattern for post-translation output.

columns.name: pre_sql_query
columns.description: PRE_SQL_QUERY from ProcessData. SQL executed in the pre-translation phase.

columns.name: comm_call_sql_query
columns.description: COMM_CALL_SQL_QUERY from ProcessData. SQL executed as part of the communication-call step.

columns.name: comm_call_cla_exe_path
columns.description: COMM_CALL_CLA_EXE_PATH from ProcessData. Path to the external executable invoked by the communication-call step. Populated presence is a FILE_PROCESS classification signal (e.g., ACADIA EO merge orchestrator, REVSPRING email scrubber).

columns.name: comm_call_working_dir
columns.description: COMM_CALL_WORKING_DIR from ProcessData. Working directory for the comm_call_cla_exe_path invocation.

columns.name: dm_batch_split
columns.description: DM_BATCH_SPLIT flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether the workflow splits its output into multiple DM batches.

columns.name: translation_staging
columns.description: TRANSLATION_STAGING flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether translation output is staged before final delivery.

columns.name: staging_cla_exe_path
columns.description: STAGING_CLA_EXE_PATH from ProcessData. Path to the external executable invoked during the staging phase.

columns.name: custom_file_filter
columns.description: CUSTOM_FILE_FILTER flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether a custom file-filter evaluator is used in place of the default.

columns.name: file_clean_up
columns.description: FILE_CLEAN_UP flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether source files are cleaned up (deleted) after successful processing.

columns.name: pre_dm_merge
columns.description: PRE_DM_MERGE flag from ProcessData. Y/N — whether a pre-DM-merge step runs before batch submission.

columns.name: etl_path
columns.description: ETL_PATH from ProcessData. Legacy ETL path field.

columns.name: log_file_path
columns.description: LOG_FILE_PATH from ProcessData. Target path for run-specific log output.

columns.name: prev_seq
columns.description: PREV_SEQ from ProcessData. SEQ_ID of the sequential predecessor run (when the client pipeline chains multiple SEQ_IDs in order). NULL for standalone SEQs.

columns.name: address_check
columns.description: ADDRESS_CHECK from ProcessData. Reserved for future expansion.

columns.name: run_class
columns.description: Collector-derived classification of what this workflow represents. See status_value rows on this column for the value catalog. Computed from CLIENT_ID, POST_TRANS_SQL_QUERY, FILE_FILTER, GET_DOCS_TYPE, PUT_DOCS_TYPE, COMM_CALL_CLA_EXE_PATH, and PROCESS_TYPE per the rules in Collect-B2BExecution.ps1 ConvertTo-RunClass.

columns.name: process_data_xml
columns.description: Full decompressed ProcessData XML captured from the first DOCUMENT TRANS_DATA row at workflow launch. Forensic safety net — preserved in case the parser needs extension against future Sterling grammar changes, without re-fetching from b2bi (which purges aggressively, ~48hr retention).

columns.name: root_cause_step_id
columns.description: STEP_ID of the earliest WORKFLOW_CONTEXT row with BASIC_STATUS not in (0, 10) — the first step where the workflow entered a non-success state. NULL when the workflow completed successfully or is still in-flight.

columns.name: root_cause_service_name
columns.description: SERVICE_NAME of the root-cause step. Identifies the Sterling service that first reported non-success status.

columns.name: root_cause_adv_status
columns.description: ADV_STATUS text from the root-cause step. Often contains stack-trace-style diagnostic detail. Stored as VARCHAR(MAX) because content can be long.

columns.name: failure_step_id
columns.description: STEP_ID of the final failing step (may differ from root_cause_step_id when a later step propagated the failure).

columns.name: failure_service_name
columns.description: SERVICE_NAME of the final failing step.

columns.name: status_message
columns.description: Collector-composed failure summary combining step, service, BASIC_STATUS, and a truncated ADV_STATUS snippet. Designed for at-a-glance display without needing to open root_cause_adv_status.

columns.name: had_trans
columns.description: Collector-computed flag. 1 when WORKFLOW_CONTEXT contains an "Inline Begin FA_CLIENTS_TRANS" ADV_STATUS marker — indicates the translation sub-workflow was invoked.

columns.name: had_vital
columns.description: Collector-computed flag. 1 when the VITAL sub-workflow was invoked during this run.

columns.name: had_accounts_load
columns.description: Collector-computed flag. 1 when the FA_CLIENTS_ACCOUNTS_LOAD sub-workflow was invoked (new-business pipeline signal).

columns.name: had_comm_call
columns.description: Collector-computed flag. 1 when the communication-call sub-workflow was invoked.

columns.name: had_archive
columns.description: Collector-computed flag. 1 when any archive sub-workflow was invoked (pre- or post-archive).

columns.name: trans_invocation_count
columns.description: Collector-computed count of FA_CLIENTS_TRANS invocations observed in WORKFLOW_CONTEXT. Acts as a proxy for the number of files processed in the run.

columns.name: archive_invocation_count
columns.description: Collector-computed count of archive sub-workflow invocations observed in WORKFLOW_CONTEXT.

columns.name: merged_output_file_name
columns.description: Reserved for Block 3 (execution detail). When the workflow produces a single merged output file, its name is captured here; NULL otherwise.

columns.name: merged_output_file_size
columns.description: Reserved for Block 3 (execution detail). Byte size of the merged output file when present.

columns.name: has_detail_captured
columns.description: Flag indicating whether the forthcoming Block 3 execution detail extraction has run for this workflow. 0 by default; set to 1 when per-file/per-creditor detail has been captured in a related detail table.

columns.name: is_complete
columns.description: Collector-derived flag. 1 when the workflow has reached a terminal state in b2bi (END_TIME populated); 0 while still in-flight. Drives the anti-join that excludes terminal rows from future collection cycles.

columns.name: completed_dttm
columns.description: Timestamp when the collector first observed the workflow in a terminal state. NULL for in-flight rows.

columns.name: completed_status
columns.description: Human-readable terminal status. See status_value rows for the value catalog. Derived from b2bi_status at completion time.

columns.name: collected_dttm
columns.description: Timestamp of the most recent INSERT or UPDATE against this row by Collect-B2BExecution.ps1.

designNotes.note: The collector evaluates each workflow exactly once in terminal state. On every cycle it queries b2bi WF_INST_S for MAIN runs in the lookback window, then anti-joins against this table's is_complete = 1 rows to exclude already-terminal workflows from further processing. Only net-new runs and still-in-flight runs get re-evaluated. This keeps per-cycle work bounded at roughly "new arrivals + currently running" rather than scaling with total table size. The partial index IX_SI_ExecutionTracking_incomplete_startTime supports the in-flight lookup directly.

designNotes.topic: ProcessData parsed structure and raw XML both retained
designNotes.note: The parsed ProcessData columns and the raw process_data_xml column are both derived from the same source — the decompressed ProcessData blob from TRANS_DATA at workflow launch. The parsed columns support fast filtered queries ("all runs with file_filter X"). The raw XML is the forensic safety net: if Sterling configuration evolves to use grammar the parser does not handle, historical rows can be re-parsed against the retained XML without re-fetching from b2bi (which has ~48 hour retention). Both representations are populated by the same collector pass so they cannot drift.

designNotes.topic: Sub-workflow detection via ADV_STATUS "Inline Begin" pattern
designNotes.note: Sterling records sub-workflow invocations in WORKFLOW_CONTEXT.ADV_STATUS as text markers like "Inline Begin FA_CLIENTS_TRANS+<ID>+<VER>" rather than in a structured column. The collector scans ADV_STATUS for these markers to compute had_trans, had_vital, had_accounts_load, had_comm_call, had_archive, trans_invocation_count, and archive_invocation_count. This approach was validated against known-good workflow types and is resilient to Sterling version bumps because it does not depend on specific WFD_ID values.

INTERNAL_OP signals are evaluated before FILE_PROCESS signals. A workflow that has both an Integration SP executor signal (e.g., POST_TRANS_SQL_QUERY starting with "EXEC Integration.") AND file-processing signals (e.g., FILE_FILTER populated) classifies as INTERNAL_OP — the SP-executor pattern is the dominant work. Only workflows with no INTERNAL_OP signal and no FILE_PROCESS signal land in UNCLASSIFIED. In production, UNCLASSIFIED should be empty in steady state; a non-zero count indicates a new workflow pattern that needs characterization.

statusValues.meaning: Standard real-client file processing — inbound pickup, outbound delivery, or external exe orchestration. Matched when FILE_FILTER, GET_DOCS_TYPE, or PUT_DOCS_TYPE is populated, or when COMM_CALL_CLA_EXE_PATH is populated, or when PROCESS_TYPE is in the push-operation whitelist (SFTP_PUSH, SFTP_PUSH_ED25519).
Workflow ran to completion without error. Corresponds to b2bi_status = 0 with END_TIME populated.

Workflow reached a terminal state but with errors. Corresponds to b2bi_status = 1 with END_TIME populated. Inspect status_message, root_cause_service_name, and root_cause_adv_status for diagnostic detail.

Integration SP executor run — the workflow's primary work is invoking an Integration stored procedure, not processing files. Matched when CLIENT_ID = 328 (INTEGRATION TOOLS pseudo-client) or when POST_TRANS_SQL_QUERY starts with "EXEC Integration." or with the XPath wrapper "concat('EXEC Integration.".

Workflow did not match any current classification rule. In steady state this bucket should be empty. A non-zero count indicates a new workflow pattern that needs investigation and a classifier extension.

Workflow reached a terminal state with a b2bi_status value other than 0 or 1 (not observed in production at time of writing). The literal pattern shown here is "STATUS_" followed by the raw numeric value; investigate any occurrence.

queries.name: Run distribution by class and completion status
queries.description: At-a-glance summary of what's been collected: how workflows split across classifications and whether they reached terminal state.
queries.sql:
SELECT run_class,
       COUNT(*) AS total_runs,
       SUM(CASE WHEN is_complete = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS completed,
       SUM(CASE WHEN is_complete = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS in_flight,
       SUM(CASE WHEN completed_status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS failed
FROM B2B.SI_ExecutionTracking
GROUP BY run_class
ORDER BY total_runs DESC;

queries.name: Recent failures with diagnostic detail
queries.description: Last 50 failed workflows with the root-cause step, service, and status message for quick triage.
queries.sql:
SELECT TOP 50
       workflow_id,
       workflow_start_time,
       client_name,
       process_type,
       comm_method,
       root_cause_step_id,
       root_cause_service_name,
       status_message
FROM B2B.SI_ExecutionTracking
WHERE completed_status = 'FAILED'
ORDER BY workflow_start_time DESC;

queries.name: Per-client volume for a given day
queries.description: Run counts and failure counts grouped by client for a specific date.
queries.sql:
SELECT client_id,
       client_name,
       COUNT(*) AS run_count,
       SUM(CASE WHEN completed_status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fail_count,
       AVG(duration_ms) AS avg_duration_ms
FROM B2B.SI_ExecutionTracking
WHERE CAST(workflow_start_time AS DATE) = CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)
GROUP BY client_id, client_name
ORDER BY run_count DESC;

queries.name: Pipeline rollup — all MAIN runs under a dispatcher
queries.description: Given a root_workflow_id (typically a dispatcher), show every MAIN run it spawned and their outcomes.
queries.sql:
SELECT workflow_id,
       workflow_start_time,
       client_name,
       process_type,
       run_class,
       completed_status,
       duration_ms
FROM B2B.SI_ExecutionTracking
WHERE root_workflow_id = @RootWorkflowId
ORDER BY workflow_start_time;

queries.name: Currently in-flight workflows
queries.description: Workflows the collector has seen start but has not yet observed terminate.
queries.sql:
SELECT workflow_id,
       workflow_start_time,
       DATEDIFF(MINUTE, workflow_start_time, GETDATE()) AS minutes_running,
       client_name,
       process_type,
       step_count
FROM B2B.SI_ExecutionTracking
WHERE is_complete = 0
ORDER BY workflow_start_time;

relationshipNotes.note: No declared FK, but the two tables correlate conceptually: SI_ScheduleRegistry describes when Sterling MAIN workflows are expected to fire, and SI_ExecutionTracking records when they actually fired.
Sole writer of this table. All INSERT and UPDATE operations against SI_ExecutionTracking originate from this collector. No other script, procedure, or Control Center route mutates this table.
On the AVG-PROD-LSNR Integration database, etl.tbl_B2B_* tables carry the structured post-workflow batch history. The workflow_id column in this table equals Integration RUN_ID.
Source table for workflow_id, workflow_start_time, workflow_end_time, b2bi_status, b2bi_state. Joined to b2bi.dbo.WFD on WFD_ID + WFD_VERSION to filter NAME = 'FA_CLIENTS_MAIN'.
Source table for parent_workflow_id and root_workflow_id. Columns C_WF_ID, P_WF_ID, and ROOT_WF_ID are queried; ROOT_WF_ID is pre-computed by Sterling so no walk-up traversal is needed.
Source table for step_count, failure diagnostic fields, and the ADV_STATUS "Inline Begin" markers that drive sub-workflow invocation flags and counts.

Source table for ProcessData — the first DOCUMENT row (REFERENCE_TABLE='DOCUMENT', PAGE_INDEX=0, ordered by CREATION_DATE ASC, DATA_ID ASC) contains the gzip-compressed ProcessData blob that yields the 73 parsed columns and process_data_xml.

tables.name: SI_ScheduleRegistry
tables.description: Master catalog of IBM Sterling B2B Integrator schedules. Stores one row per SCHEDULEID from b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE with parsed TIMINGXML structure and a human-readable schedule description. The collector fully synchronizes this table with b2bi on each cycle: new schedules are inserted, changed schedules are re-parsed and updated, and schedules no longer present in b2bi are removed. This table is the authoritative source for "what schedules exist in Sterling, when they run, and their current operational status" for the xFACts B2B module.
tables.dataFlow: Collect-B2BExecution.ps1 fully synchronizes this table with b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE on every collection cycle. The collector queries all rows from SCHEDULE, fetches each TIMINGXML blob from b2bi.dbo.DATA_TABLE, decompresses the gzip content, parses the XML into the structured columns (run_day_mask, run_times_explicit, run_range_start/end, run_interval_minutes, excluded_dates, etc.), generates the human-readable schedule_description, and MERGEs the result. New schedules are INSERTed; existing schedules are re-parsed if the timing_xml_handle has changed; schedules no longer present in b2bi are DELETEd from the registry. The Control Center B2B page (future) reads this table to display the schedule modal/panel. No other xFACts components currently read this table; it will become a join target for SI_ExecutionTracking in Phase 3 Block 2 to correlate observed workflow runs against their expected schedules.

columns.name: schedule_id
columns.description: Primary key. SCHEDULEID value from b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE. Immutable identifier assigned by Sterling.

columns.name: service_name
columns.description: SERVICENAME from b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE. Name of the Sterling workflow (business process) this schedule fires. Examples: "FA_CLIENTS_GET_LIST", "FA_AMSURG_MHS_IB_BD_NB", "BackupService".

columns.name: schedule_type
columns.description: SCHEDULETYPE from b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE. Sterling-internal integer classifying schedule type. Semantics defined by Sterling, not xFACts; captured for lineage and future correlation.

columns.name: schedule_type_id
columns.description: SCHEDULETYPEID from b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE. Sterling-internal secondary classifier. Semantics defined by Sterling; captured for lineage.

columns.name: execution_timer
columns.description: EXECUTIONTIMER from b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE. Sterling-internal execution mode flag. Has three distinct observed values across all active schedules (0, 1, 2) — value 1 is dominant. Semantics defined by Sterling; captured for classification research.

columns.name: source_status
columns.description: STATUS value from b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE. Sterling-managed state of the schedule itself — not xFACts-managed. Observed values include ACTIVE, HOLD. Pass-through from Sterling; interpret against Sterling documentation if needed.

columns.name: execution_status
columns.description: EXECUTIONSTATUS value from b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE. Sterling scheduler state for the schedule's execution pipeline — not xFACts-managed. Typical value observed is WAIT. Pass-through from Sterling.

columns.name: timing_xml_handle
columns.description: TIMINGXML handle from b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE — a DATA_ID pointer into b2bi.dbo.DATA_TABLE where the gzip-compressed TIMINGXML blob is stored. Captured for lineage traceability; the collector decompresses this at collection time and stores the result in timing_xml.

columns.name: source_system_name
columns.description: SYSTEMNAME from b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE. Sterling cluster node that owns the schedule (e.g., "node1"). Relevant if Sterling is ever run multi-node.

columns.name: source_user_id
columns.description: USERID from b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE. User account that created or owns the schedule in Sterling (typically "admin").

columns.name: timing_pattern_type
columns.description: Derived classifier for the schedule's timing pattern. See status_value entries for valid values and their meanings. Used for UI filtering and classification. Derived by collector from timing_xml structure.

columns.name: run_day_mask
columns.description: CHAR(7) bitmap-style string representing days of week the schedule runs. Position order: Sun-Mon-Tue-Wed-Thu-Fri-Sat. Each position is either the first letter of that day (S/M/T/W/T/F/S) if the schedule runs that day, or a dash (-) if it does not. Examples: "SMTWTFS" = every day (ofWeek=-1), "-MTWTF-" = Mon-Fri, "S-----S" = Sat and Sun. NULL when timing_pattern_type is MONTHLY (ofMonth only).

columns.name: run_days_of_month
columns.description: Comma-delimited list of days of month the schedule runs, when ofMonth is used in TIMINGXML. Example: "1,15" for a twice-monthly schedule. NULL when the schedule uses ofWeek instead.

columns.name: run_times_explicit
columns.description: Comma-delimited list of explicit HH:MM times the schedule runs, when the TIMINGXML contains <time> elements. Example: "05:00,06:00,07:00,...,18:00" for a schedule with 14 hourly fire times. NULL when the schedule uses <timeRange> instead.

columns.name: run_interval_minutes
columns.description: Interval in minutes between runs, parsed from <interval> inside <timeRange>. Example: 60 for hourly. NULL for schedules using explicit <time> entries.

columns.name: run_range_start
columns.description: HH:MM start of the run window, parsed from the first four chars of <range> inside <timeRange>. Example: "05:05". NULL for schedules using explicit <time> entries.

columns.name: run_range_end
columns.description: HH:MM end of the run window, parsed from the last four chars of <range> inside <timeRange>. Example: "15:05". NULL for schedules using explicit <time> entries.

columns.name: run_on_minute
columns.description: Value from <onMinute> inside <timeRange>, captured for lineage completeness. Observed value is 0 for virtually every schedule; the actual minute marker of fire times is derived from run_range_start. Retained for forensic purposes.

columns.name: excluded_dates
columns.description: Comma-delimited list of MM-DD dates the schedule skips, when <excludedDates> is populated. Example: "01-01,12-25" for a schedule that excludes New Year's Day and Christmas. NULL when no exclusions configured.

columns.name: first_run_time_of_day
columns.description: HH:MM of the earliest run time on any active day, derived from either the minimum <time> value or the start of the <timeRange>. Useful for "will this run in the next hour" queries. NULL only when parsing cannot determine a value (i.e., timing_pattern_type = UNKNOWN).

columns.name: last_run_time_of_day
columns.description: HH:MM of the latest run time on any active day. Useful for "when is the last fire of the day" queries. NULL only when timing_pattern_type = UNKNOWN.

columns.name: expected_runs_per_day
columns.description: Total number of times per day this schedule is expected to fire. For explicit-time patterns, count of <time> entries. For interval patterns, derived from range span and interval. Useful for volume planning and Phase 4 schedule-adherence monitoring.

columns.name: schedule_description
columns.description: Human-readable summary generated at parse time. Intended for Control Center display (schedule modal) and ad-hoc query results. Examples: "Daily at 04:00", "Mon-Fri at 14:00", "Every 60 min at :05, 05:05-15:05, Mon-Fri (excl. 01-01, 12-25)", "Days 1,15 of month at 09:00".

columns.name: timing_xml
columns.description: Full decompressed TIMINGXML content from b2bi.dbo.DATA_TABLE, captured at collection time. Stored raw as the forensic safety net: if the grammar evolves beyond what the parser handles, or the parser has a bug, the raw content is preserved for re-parse without re-fetching from b2bi (which purges aggressively).

columns.name: last_modified_dttm
columns.description: Timestamp of the most recent change to any column on this row. Set to GETDATE() on INSERT by default; updated by the collector on every detected change.

designNotes.summary: The decompressed TIMINGXML is always stored inline in the timing_xml column alongside the parsed structural columns. This is a deliberate redundancy choice.
designNotes.note: The grammar variety observed across 506 active schedules in b2bi produced 73 distinct structural patterns. The parser handles the documented grammar (timingxml/TimingXML root elements, ofWeek/ofMonth day specifiers, <time> and <timeRange> time specifiers, excludedDates), but Sterling has been observed to evolve schedule configurations over time, and a future schedule may introduce grammar that the parser does not recognize. By capturing the raw decompressed XML on every row, the forensic record is preserved regardless of parser coverage. If the parser ever needs to be extended, historical rows can be re-parsed against the same timing_xml column without re-fetching from b2bi, which purges aggressively.

designNotes.summary: The collector fully synchronizes this table with b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE on each cycle. Schedules that disappear from b2bi are DELETEd from this registry rather than soft-marked.
designNotes.note: Schedules are master data, not operational history. The registry's purpose is to represent "what is configured in Sterling right now" for join and display purposes. If schedule X is removed from Sterling, we do not need to retain a row here — execution history is tracked separately in SI_ExecutionTracking via captured WORKFLOW_ID values, which are independent of the schedule registry. A DELETE on this table does not lose anything that matters, and it keeps the table lean with no lifecycle audit columns (no first_seen, last_seen, is_deleted_in_source, deleted_in_source_dttm). If operational interest in "when did schedule X go away" ever arises, that tracking belongs in a different table with explicit audit semantics.

designNotes.summary: The run_day_mask column uses a fixed-width CHAR(7) string where each position represents a day of the week, rather than separate BIT columns or an integer bitmap.
designNotes.note: Position order is Sun-Mon-Tue-Wed-Thu-Fri-Sat. Each position is either the first letter of that day (S/M/T/W/T/F/S) when the schedule runs that day, or a dash (-) when it does not. This format is directly human-readable in ad-hoc queries (a Mon-Fri schedule shows as "-MTWTF-") and supports fast LIKE-based filtering (e.g., "schedules that run Monday" becomes WHERE run_day_mask LIKE '_M_____'). Seven separate BIT columns would have been more normalized but produce wider rows and noisier query output; an integer bitmap would have been more compact but unreadable without decoding. CHAR(7) with letter-or-dash is the readable compromise.

designNotes.topic: Parsed Structure vs. Raw XML: Intent
designNotes.summary: The parsed structural columns (run_day_mask, run_times_explicit, run_range_start/end, run_interval_minutes, excluded_dates) and the schedule_description column are both derived from timing_xml at parse time.
designNotes.note: Why duplicate the information? Different access patterns. Structured columns support fast queries ("which schedules run in the next hour", "which schedules are weekday-only", "which schedules exclude holidays"). The schedule_description supports display — Control Center modal, report output, ad-hoc investigation — without requiring UI-side parse logic. Raw timing_xml supports forensic re-parse if the grammar evolves. All three views of the same information, each serving a distinct use case. Because the source of truth is b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE and the collector owns all derivation, the three representations cannot drift.

designNotes.summary: The run_on_minute column captures the <onMinute> value from TIMINGXML timeRange patterns, but observed values across all active schedules are uniformly 0.
designNotes.note: The <onMinute> element appears in every <timeRange> block in every observed schedule, always with value 0. The actual minute marker of fire times (e.g., 05:05 hourly firing at :05 past each hour) is anchored by the minute portion of the range_start value, not by onMinute. This appears to be a Sterling grammar element that is either vestigial, reserved for future use, or overridden by the range definition in practice. The column is retained for lineage completeness, but the schedule_description generator uses the range_start minute rather than run_on_minute to describe the actual fire pattern.

designNotes.topic: Columns Dropped From Source SCHEDULE Table
designNotes.summary: Nine columns from b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE are intentionally not captured in this table.
designNotes.note: Dropped columns (all verified constant across 506 active schedules): PARAMS (never populated), EXECUTIONDATE (always 0), EXECUTIONHOUR (always 0), EXECUTIONMINUTE (always 0), EXECUTIONCOUNT (always -1), EXECUTIONCURCOUNT (always 0), ORGANIZATIONKEY (always a single space character — a Sterling multi-tenancy field not used at FAC), XAPIMETHOD (always NULL), XAPIXML (always NULL). Capturing them would add width with no information value. If any of these ever becomes populated with meaningful data in the future, the raw timing_xml column does not help — these live directly on the SCHEDULE row, not in TIMINGXML. They would need to be added to both the table schema and the collector projection. Low-risk given their observed uniformity, but flagged here for future review if Sterling behavior changes.

statusValues.meaning: Schedule runs every day (ofWeek="-1"), one or more explicit <time> fire points per day. Most common pattern; covers schedules like "Daily at 04:00" or "Daily at 05:00, 06:00, ..., 18:00".
Schedule runs on specific days of the week (ofWeek="1" through "7") with one or more explicit <time> fire points. Covers patterns like "Mon-Fri at 14:00" or "Sundays at 11:00".
Schedule runs on specific days of the month (ofMonth="N") with one or more explicit <time> fire points. Covers patterns like "1st of month at 10:41" or "1st and 15th at 09:00".

Schedule uses <timeRange> pattern with <interval> and <onMinute> — fires every N minutes within a defined HH:MM-HH:MM window. Covers patterns like "Every 60 min, 05:35-23:35, daily" and "Every 5 min, 00:00-23:59, daily".
Schedule uses <timeRange> pattern on specific days of week. Example: the FA_CLIENTS_GET_LIST schedule fires every 60 min from 05:05-15:05 on Mon-Fri only.

TIMINGXML content did not match any of the documented grammar patterns the parser handles. The raw XML is captured in timing_xml for inspection and parser extension. Rows with this classification should be investigated — either the grammar has evolved or the parser has a gap.

scripts.name: Collect-B2BExecution.ps1
scripts.description: Collector for the B2B module. Two workloads each cycle: (1) synchronizes B2B.SI_ScheduleRegistry from b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE — inserts new schedules, updates changed ones, deletes schedules no longer in b2bi; (2) collects per-workflow execution tracking rows into B2B.SI_ExecutionTracking for FA_CLIENTS_MAIN runs in the lookback window (GlobalConfig b2b_collect_lookback_days, default 3) — inserts new workflows, updates still-in-flight ones, skips already-terminal rows via is_complete anti-join. Reads exclusively from b2bi on FA-INT-DBP and writes to the xFACts AG. Runs under the orchestrator in FIRE_AND_FORGET mode.
scripts.dataFlow:
Block 1 (schedule sync): Single JOIN query against b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE and b2bi.dbo.DATA_TABLE on FA-INT-DBP fetches all schedule rows with their gzip-compressed TIMINGXML blobs. Each blob is decompressed in-memory and parsed into the structured columns and human-readable schedule_description. The collector MERGEs against the existing registry: INSERTs new schedules, UPDATEs schedules whose timing_xml_handle / source_status / execution_status have changed, and DELETEs registry rows whose schedule_id no longer appears in b2bi.

Block 2 (execution collection): Reads the lookback window from GlobalConfig (b2b_collect_lookback_days). Queries b2bi.dbo.WF_INST_S joined to b2bi.dbo.WFD on WFD_ID + WFD_VERSION filtered by NAME = 'FA_CLIENTS_MAIN' within the window. Loads hash sets of already-terminal workflow_ids and already-in-flight workflow_ids from B2B.SI_ExecutionTracking. For each b2bi row not in the terminal set, the collector: queries WORKFLOW_LINKAGE for parent/root IDs, fetches and decompresses the first DOCUMENT TRANS_DATA row for ProcessData (passing -MaxBinaryLength 20971520 to avoid silent 1024-byte truncation), parses the 73 ProcessData fields, scans WORKFLOW_CONTEXT for step counts and ADV_STATUS "Inline Begin" sub-workflow markers, derives run_class, determines terminal state from END_TIME/STATUS, and INSERTs (new) or UPDATEs (still-in-flight) the row. GlobalConfig values b2b_alerting_enabled and b2b_collect_lookback_days are read at startup. Orchestration context (TaskId, ProcessId) is passed in by the engine; on completion Complete-OrchestratorTask updates Orchestrator.TaskLog and Orchestrator.ProcessRegistry.

designNotes.note: The script performs both Block 1 (schedule sync) and Block 2 (execution collection) in every cycle. Rather than splitting into two separate scripts, the single-collector design keeps the orchestrator footprint minimal, shares all the b2bi connection and parsing machinery, and lets the two workloads benefit from the same GlobalConfig, logging, and RBAC setup. Both workloads are independent — a failure in one does not block the other. Execution order is Block 1 first (faster, builds the registry used for future schedule-adherence checks), then Block 2 (the long-running workload).

designNotes.topic: is_complete anti-join for bounded per-cycle work
designNotes.note: Block 2 would scale linearly with lookback window size if every MAIN workflow were re-evaluated each cycle. The is_complete anti-join pattern bounds work to "new arrivals + still-in-flight" by loading hash sets of terminal and in-flight workflow_ids from SI_ExecutionTracking and skipping any b2bi row already in the terminal set. Terminal rows are never re-read from b2bi; in-flight rows are refreshed until they go terminal, then become dormant. Partial index IX_SI_ExecutionTracking_incomplete_startTime supports the in-flight lookup with minimal memory footprint.

designNotes.topic: Name-based workflow filter survives version bumps
designNotes.note: Sterling assigns a new WFD_ID to every published version of a workflow definition. Filtering by WFD_ID directly would break every time the FA_CLIENTS_MAIN workflow gets republished. The collector instead joins WF_INST_S to WFD on WFD_ID + WFD_VERSION and filters by WFD.NAME = 'FA_CLIENTS_MAIN'. This is resilient to version bumps and matches the actual lookup pattern used across the documented query library.

b2bi stores ProcessData and TIMINGXML as gzip-compressed VARBINARY(MAX). The collector passes -MaxBinaryLength 20971520 (20MB) on every VARBINARY fetch — both Block 1 schedule TIMINGXML and Block 2 ProcessData — via the matching parameter on Get-SqlData.

Sub-workflow invocations within a FA_CLIENTS_MAIN run are recorded in WORKFLOW_CONTEXT.ADV_STATUS as text markers like "Inline Begin FA_CLIENTS_TRANS+<ID>+<VER>" rather than in a structured column. The collector scans ADV_STATUS using pattern matching to populate had_trans, had_vital, had_accounts_load, had_comm_call, had_archive, and the two invocation count columns. This text-based detection is resilient to Sterling version bumps because it does not depend on specific WFD_IDs.

ConvertTo-RunClass evaluates INTERNAL_OP signals before FILE_PROCESS signals. A workflow with both an Integration SP executor signal (POST_TRANS_SQL_QUERY starting with "EXEC Integration." or "concat('EXEC Integration.") AND a file-process signal (FILE_FILTER, GET_DOCS_TYPE, PUT_DOCS_TYPE, COMM_CALL_CLA_EXE_PATH, or PROCESS_TYPE in the push whitelist) is classified INTERNAL_OP — the SP executor is the dominant work pattern. Only workflows matching neither signal set land in UNCLASSIFIED.

Terminal state is detected from END_TIME populated on WF_INST_S. Sterling WF_INST_S.STATUS = 0 means completed successfully; STATUS = 1 means terminated with errors. STATE is a separate numeric state code observed uniformly as 1 on terminal rows and is not consulted by the collector — the completed_status column derives strictly from STATUS.

relationshipNotes.note: This is the sole writer of B2B.SI_ScheduleRegistry. The collector issues per-row INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE statements against this table based on diff with b2bi.dbo.SCHEDULE.

The collector is registered in Orchestrator.ProcessRegistry as module B2B, process Collect-B2BExecution. Scheduling and run mode are controlled there; runtime status fields (running_count, last_execution_status, last_duration_ms) are updated by the Complete-OrchestratorTask callback at the end of each run. dependency_group bucket = 10 (collectors).

Dot-sourced at script startup. Provides Initialize-XFActsScript for SQL module loading and application identity tagging, Get-SqlData and Invoke-SqlNonQuery for database access (including -MaxBinaryLength for VARBINARY blob reads from b2bi TRANS_DATA), and Complete-OrchestratorTask for the orchestrator completion callback.

Sole writer of B2B.SI_ExecutionTracking. Each cycle INSERTs new FA_CLIENTS_MAIN workflows discovered in b2bi within the lookback window, UPDATEs in-flight rows from prior cycles, and skips rows with is_complete = 1.

Reads from b2bi. All cross-database correlation happens in PowerShell memory. Reads: dbo.SCHEDULE, dbo.DATA_TABLE (TIMINGXML and ProcessData blobs), dbo.WF_INST_S, dbo.WFD, dbo.WORKFLOW_LINKAGE, dbo.WORKFLOW_CONTEXT, dbo.TRANS_DATA. b2bi retention drives the lookback and the decision to retain ProcessData XML.
